250	theorem higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
251	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
252	    (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) :
253	    IsZero (higherDirectImage j q H) := by
254	  -- `j` is an affine morphism (open immersion of an affine into a separated scheme),
255	  -- so the preimage of every affine open `V ⊆ X` is the affine open `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V`.
256	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
257	  -- Presheaf description (Stacks 01XJ): `Rᵠ j_* H ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hᵠ((j_* I•)(V)))`
258	  -- for any injective resolution `I` of `H`. Reduce to the vanishing of that sheafification.
259	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
260	    (higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology j q (injectiveResolution H))
261	  set α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
262	  set P := (pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H)).homology q with hP
263	  -- Reflect `IsZero` through the faithful, zero-preserving forgetful functor `toSheaf`.
264	  apply Functor.isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf)
265	  -- Transport across the sheafification square
266	  -- `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ toPresheaf`.
267	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
268	  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
269	  -- KEY VANISHING (genuine cohomological gap): the presheaf homology `Q` is a *zero object*
270	  -- over every affine open `W ⊆ X`, because `j⁻¹W` is affine (`j` is an affine morphism) and
271	  -- `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)` for `q ≥ 1`.
272	  have hSec : ∀ (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X), IsAffineOpen W →
273	      IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op W)) := by
274	    intro W hW
275	    -- Evaluate-at-`W` the presheaf complex; this functor preserves homology, so
276	    -- `Q.obj (op W) ≅ Hᵠ(Γ(W, f_* I•)) = Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`, which vanishes for affine `j⁻¹W`.
277	    set GW := PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
278	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W) with hGW
279	    set Kp := pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H) with hKp
280	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ (GW.mapHomologyIso' (ComplexShape.up ℕ) Kp q).symm
281	    -- The section complex `n ↦ Γ(W, f_* I^n) = Γ(j⁻¹W, I^n)` is the image of the chosen
282	    -- injective resolution of `H` under the additive sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor.
283	    have hcomplex : (GW.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj Kp
284	        = ((pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
285	            (injectiveResolution H).cocomplex :=
286	      rfl
287	    rw [hcomplex]
288	    -- `isoRightDerivedObj` recognises this homology as the `q`-th right derived sections
289	    -- functor `Rᵠ Γ(j⁻¹W, -)` applied to `H`, i.e. the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
290	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((injectiveResolution H).isoRightDerivedObj
291	      (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W) q).symm
292	    -- The sections functor `Γ(j⁻¹W, -)` (here `pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W`, which is
293	    -- definitionally `sectionsFunctor (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)`) is corepresented by `jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)`, so its
294	    -- `q`-th right derived functor agrees with that of `Hom(jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W), -)`.  This
295	    -- discharges the *geometric* corepresentability half of Bridge (1)/(2).
296	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((rightDerivedNatIso (sectionsFunctorCorepIso (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) q).app H)
297	    -- RESIDUAL (Bridge 1/2 remainder + Bridge 2 vanishing): the `q`-th right derived functor of
298	    -- `Hom(jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W), -)` vanishes on the quasi-coherent `H` for `q ≥ 1`.  Two pieces
299	    -- remain (see the task-result hand-off): (i) the homological-algebra identification
300	    -- `(preadditiveCoyoneda (op P)).rightDerived q ≅ Ext^q(P, -)` via the Hom-complex of an
301	    -- injective resolution (`InjectiveResolution.extAddEquivCohomologyClass` ∘ `homologyAddEquiv`),
302	    -- turning this into `IsZero (Ext (jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) H q)`; and (ii) Serre vanishing for that
303	    -- `Ext` over the affine open `j ⁻¹ᵁ W` (a change-of-scheme transport of
304	    -- `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`/`affine_serre_vanishing` to a `BasisCovSystem` on the affine
305	    -- scheme `U`).
306	    sorry
307	  -- Since affine opens form a basis of `X`, every section of `Q` restricts to `0` on the
308	  -- affine opens contained in its domain, so `Q` is sectionwise locally zero and its
309	  -- sheafification vanishes.
310	  apply CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero
311	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Q := Q)
312	  intro U s
313	  refine ⟨{ arrows := fun {V} _ => ∃ W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X,
314	              IsAffineOpen W ∧ V ≤ W ∧ W ≤ U,
315	            downward_closed := ?_ }, ?_, ?_⟩
316	  · rintro V V' g ⟨A, hA, hVA, hAU⟩ h
317	    exact ⟨A, hA, le_trans (leOfHom h) hVA, hAU⟩
318	  · -- membership in the opens topology: every point of `U` has an affine basis neighbourhood
319	    intro x hx
320	    obtain ⟨W, hWmem, hxW, hWU⟩ :=
321	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp (Scheme.isBasis_affineOpens X)) hx
322	    exact ⟨W, homOfLE hWU, ⟨W, hWmem, le_refl W, hWU⟩, hxW⟩
323	  · -- the restriction of `s` to a sieve member `V ≤ A ≤ U` (with `A` affine) factors through
324	    -- `Q.obj (op A) = 0`, hence vanishes.
325	    rintro V g ⟨A, hA, hVA, hAU⟩
326	    haveI : Subsingleton (ToType (Q.obj (Opposite.op A))) :=
327	      AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hSec A hA)
328	    have hgfac : g.op = (homOfLE hAU).op ≫ (homOfLE hVA).op := by
329	      rw [← op_comp]; congr 1
330	    rw [hgfac, Q.map_comp, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
331	      Subsingleton.elim ((ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map (homOfLE hAU).op)) s) 0, map_zero]

333	/-- **Composition formula for higher direct images across an open immersion** (pinned
334	declaration; blueprint `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`, part (2); Stacks
335	`lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`).
336	
337	Let `j : U ↪ X` be an open immersion of an affine open `U` into a separated scheme `X`,
338	and `f : X ⟶ S` any morphism. For every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H` and every `k ≥ 0`:
339	```
340	  R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ R^k (f ∘ j)_* H.
341	```
342	Proof route: choose an injective resolution `H → I•` in `U.Modules`; apply `j_*`
343	degreewise to get an `f_*`-acyclic resolution `j_* I•` of `j_* H`
344	(`higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` for `j_*`-acyclicity, extended to
345	`f_*`-acyclicity via the same Serre-vanishing argument on `U ∩ f⁻¹V`); then
346	`Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` with `G = (pushforward f)` and the identity
347	`(pushforward f) ∘ (pushforward j) = pushforward (j ≫ f)` give the stated isomorphism. -/
348	noncomputable def higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp
349	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
350	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
351	    (f : X ⟶ S) (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (k : ℕ) :
352	    higherDirectImage f k ((pushforward j).obj H) ≅
353	      higherDirectImage (j ≫ f) k H := by
354	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
355	  -- Structural plan (all categorical building blocks below are verified to exist):
356	  --   • `G := Scheme.Modules.pushforward f` is additive and preserves finite limits
357	  --     (`inferInstance`), so `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` applies to it.
358	  --   • The pushed-forward injective resolution `K := (pushforward j).mapHomologicalComplex I•`
359	  --     is a candidate acyclic resolution of `j_* H = (pushforward j).obj H`.
360	  --   • `pushforwardComp j f : pushforward j ⋙ pushforward f ≅ pushforward (j ≫ f)` turns
361	  --     `H^k(G(K))` into `H^k((j ≫ f)_* I•) = R^k (j ≫ f)_* H`.
362	  -- RESIDUAL (genuine cohomological gaps, handed off — each depends on Part (1)'s residual):
363	  --   (a) `K` is exact in positive degrees and resolves `j_* H` (i.e. `j_* I•` is a
364	  --       resolution of `j_* H`) — this is `j_*`-acyclicity, the content of
365	  --       `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` applied to each `Iⁿ` and to `H`.
366	  --   (b) each term `(pushforward j).obj (Iⁿ)` is `(pushforward f)`-acyclic
367	  --       (`Functor.IsRightAcyclic`), i.e. `R^k f_*(j_* Iⁿ) = 0` for `k ≥ 1` — the same
368	  --       Serre-vanishing argument on the affine `U ∩ f⁻¹V` (generalises Part (1) from
369	  --       `j_*` to `f_*` via the presheaf description on `f⁻¹V`).
370	  -- Then `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution (pushforward f) K (j_* H) e (a) k`
371	  -- composed with the `pushforwardComp` transport gives the stated iso.
372	  sorry

content:
# Analogy: transporting an Ext / right-derived cohomology computation across a scheme isomorphism

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
change-of-scheme-cohomology

## Iteration
056

## Question
Find Mathlib's idiom (decl names + imports) for transporting the right-derived / `Ext` cohomology
computation `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, H)` across the canonical iso of an affine scheme with `Spec` of its
global sections, so the ⊤-case Serre vanishing `affine_serre_vanishing` over `Spec R` discharges the
residual `IsZero ((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)))).rightDerived q).obj H)` for
`j⁻¹W` a *general affine open* of the affine scheme `U`. Is the transport route sound and cheaper
than building a `BasisCovSystem U` over arbitrary affine opens?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/OpenImmersionPushforward.lean:296-306` — the residual `sorry`
  (`hSec` leaf inside `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic`); already reduced through
  `rightDerivedNatIso (sectionsFunctorCorepIso (j ⁻¹ᵁ W))` to
  `IsZero ((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)))).rightDerived q).obj H)`.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/AffineSerreVanishing.lean:521` — `affine_serre_vanishing`, the
  available ⊤-case (`Ext^p (jShriekOU ⊤) F = 0` over `Spec R`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechToCohomology.lean:323,429` — `BasisCovSystem` and
  `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (the comparison consuming a cover system on a basis `B`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/AffineSerreVanishing.lean:373` — `affineCoverSystem R`
  (`BasisCovSystem (Spec R)` with `B = {D f}`).
- `analogies/restriction-preserves-injectives.md` (iter-026) — the project's recorded decision to
  adopt **Form B** *precisely to avoid* restriction-preserves-injectives (a 200–500 LOC Mathlib gap).

## Headline finding (read this first)

The directive's chosen route — **make `V = j⁻¹W` the whole space via `V ≅ Spec Γ(V,𝒪)`** — is a
**WALL, not a shortcut.** It secretly re-introduces **restriction-preserves-injectives**, the exact
Mathlib gap the project deliberately side-stepped in iter-026 by adopting Form B
(`H^q(V,−) := Ext^q_{U.Modules}(jShriekOU V, −)`, ambient, no restriction). The transport is *not*
circular, but its cost is the 200–500 LOC `j_!`/restriction-injectives infrastructure, with the same
risk profile that already forced a pivot once.

Reason (the crux): `jShriekOU_U V` and `H` both live in `U.Modules`, and the only available iso is
`V ≅ Spec Γ(V)` — an iso of the **open subscheme** `V`, **not** of `U`. To use it you must first
**restrict** `U.Modules → V.Modules` (`H ↦ H|_V`); the derived comparison
`Ext^q_{U.Modules}(j_!𝒪_V, H) ≅ H^q(V, H|_V)` *is* restriction-preserves-injectives (an injective
resolution in `U.Modules` must stay injective after `(−)|_V` to compute the intrinsic `H^q(V,−)`).
`V ↪ U` is an **open immersion, not an iso**, so there is no equivalence `U.Modules ≌ V.Modules` to
transport `Ext` along — only the non-exact-inverse restriction functor.

**Contrast:** `U ≅ Spec Γ(U)` (the *whole* affine `U`) IS a genuine iso, giving a genuine
equivalence `U.Modules ≌ (Spec ΓU).Modules`. That transport ("need #1", abstract-affine → `Spec`)
is sound. But it sends the proper open `V ⊆ U` to a **general (non-distinguished) affine open**
`φ(V) ⊆ Spec ΓU`, *not* to `⊤`. So even after the sound whole-affine transport, you still face
general-affine-open vanishing, which `affine_serre_vanishing` (⊤ only) does not cover.

**The general-affine-open gap is unavoidable** (no basis dodge): `Q.obj(op W) = H^q(j⁻¹W, H)` and any
affine open `W ⊆ X` straddling the boundary of `j(U)` forces `j⁻¹W = U ⊓ W` to be a general affine
open of `U`; points of `W` on the boundary of `j(U)` cannot be covered by opens whose `j`-preimage is
distinguished-or-empty. So the residual genuinely is "Serre vanishing for **every** affine open of an
affine scheme".

**The sound, Form-B-native discharge** is to stay ambient and get general-affine-open vanishing from
`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` by **enlarging the cover system's basis `B` from `{D f}` to all affine
opens** — `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis s … V hV` already gives `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, H)=0` for *any*
`V ∈ s.B`, entirely inside `(Spec R).Modules`, with no restriction functor anywhere in its proof.

## Verified Lean names

### Q1 — affine scheme ≅ Spec(Γ)
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isoSpec (X) [IsAffine X] : X ≅ Spec (X.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))`
  — `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme`. **[verified]**
- `Scheme.isoSpec_hom : X.isoSpec.hom = X.toSpecΓ` **[verified]**;
  `Scheme.toSpecΓ_isoSpec_inv` **[verified]**;
  `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_top : hV.fromSpec = X.isoSpec.inv` **[verified]**.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isCompact (hU : IsAffineOpen U) : IsCompact ↑U`
  — `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme`. **[verified]** (this is what generalizes
  `standard_cover_cofinal` from `D f` to a general affine open).

### Q2 — module-category functoriality from a scheme morphism / iso
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforward (f : X ⟶ Y) : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules`
  — `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf:151`. **[verified]**
- `Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _` (`:190`),
  `Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp : pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g)` (`:210`),
  `Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g` (`:224`),
  instance `(pushforward f).Additive` (`:182`),
  `Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` (`:172`). **[verified, all in Modules/Sheaf.lean]**
- **[gap — assembly]** Mathlib has **no packaged** `Y.Modules ≌ Z.Modules` from a scheme iso. It is
  assembled from `pushforward φ.hom` / `pushforward φ.inv` + `pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr`/
  `pushforwardId` (the unit/counit isos), ~30–60 LOC. Sound and Mathlib-aligned (a genuine
  equivalence because `φ` is an iso).

### Q3 — getting `Ext` to transport across the equivalence
- `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mapExactFunctor (F) [F exact] : Ext X Y n → Ext (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) n`
  — `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Map.lean:126`; with
  `Functor.mapExtAddHom` (`:169`), `Ext.mapExactFunctor_zero` (`:160`),
  `Ext.mapExactFunctor_comp`. **[verified]** An equivalence is exact, so `mapExactFunctor` of the
  equivalence (and of its inverse) gives the `Ext`-iso. This is a *cleaner* vehicle than
  `rightDerivedNatIso` for the whole-affine transport: `e : Ext_{U}(jShriekOU V, H) ↦
  e.mapExactFunctor Φ.functor` lands in the `Spec ΓU` `Ext`, which the enlarged cover system kills;
  injectivity of `mapExactFunctor` for an equivalence sends `e ↦ 0`.
- Already-built project vehicles (keep, reusable for either need): `rightDerivedNatIso`,
  `sectionsFunctorCorepIso` (`OpenImmersionPushforward.lean:156,174`). They connect
  `(preadditiveCoyoneda (op (jShriekOU V))).rightDerived q` ≅ `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, −)`. They are
  **not** the wall.

## Decisions identified

### Decision: route for *general-affine-open* `Ext`-vanishing (need #2) — isoSpec-of-`V` vs enlarge-`B`
- **Mathlib idiom**: for "cohomology of every member of a basis", build the basis into the comparison
  input. The project already has the right vehicle: `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis (s) … V hV` gives
  `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, H)=0` for any `V ∈ s.B`. The Mathlib-idiomatic move is to make `B` the right
  set, not to change spaces. (`CechToCohomology.lean:429`.) **[verified]**
- **Project's proposed path (directive)**: transport the ⊤-case along `V ≅ Spec Γ(V)`.
- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (as a *cheaper* route). It forces restriction-preserves-injectives —
  a 200–500 LOC Mathlib gap-fill the project already rejected (`restriction-preserves-injectives.md`).
- **Cost of divergence**: build `j_!` functor / restriction-preserves-injectives (200–500+ LOC, HIGH
  risk; the project pivoted away from this once already). The ambient enlarge-`B` alternative is
  ~40–80 LOC.
- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** for the isoSpec-of-`V` route (do NOT take it). **ALIGN /
  PROCEED** on enlarging `B`.

### Decision: enlarge `affineCoverSystem` basis `B` from `{D f}` to all affine opens (the recommended need #2)
- **Mathlib idiom / mechanism**: `BasisCovSystem` is generic over `B`/`Cov`; `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`
  consumes any `V ∈ B`. Set `B := { affine opens }`, keep `Cov` = standard covers `i ↦ D(g i)` but
  let the covered set be any affine open `V = ⨆ D(g i)`.
- **What must be re-proved**:
  - `faces_mem`: faces `⨅ D(g_{σk}) = D(∏ g)` are distinguished ⊆ affine — **already covered**, even
    easier than before (`affine_faces_mem`).
  - `injective_acyclic`: cover-agnostic (`injective_cech_acyclicFam`) — **unchanged**.
  - `surj_of_vanishing`: now needed for every affine open `V ∈ B`. Generalize `affine_surj_of_vanishing`
    (`AffineSerreVanishing.lean:233`, currently `f : R`, `V = D f`) to a general affine open. Its proof
    uses only quasi-compactness of the target + the basic-open basis, via `standard_cover_cofinal`
    (`:167`); swap `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f` for `hV.isCompact`
    (`IsAffineOpen.isCompact` **[verified]**). **This is the one genuine new piece.**
- **Cost**: ~40–80 LOC, low risk (mechanical reindex of two shipped lemmas + the structure literal).
- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** (recommended discharge of need #2; fully ambient, Form-B-native, zero new
  Mathlib gaps).

### Decision: abstract-affine `U` → `Spec Γ(U)` transport (need #1, UNAVOIDABLE)
- `U` is `[IsAffine U]`, not literally `Spec R`, while every affine-development decl is over `Spec R`.
  So a transport along `U.isoSpec : U ≅ Spec Γ(U)` is required no matter which need-#2 route is taken.
- **Mathlib idiom**: equivalence from `isoSpec` via `pushforward`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr`/
  `pushforwardId`; transport `Ext` via `Ext.mapExactFunctor`. Side obligations: `Φ.functor (jShriekOU_U V)
  ≅ jShriekOU (φ ''ᵁ V)` (jShriekOU is yoneda+sheafify on `Opens`, so an iso of schemes transports it),
  `φ ''ᵁ V` affine, `Φ.functor H` quasi-coherent. All standard, all sound (genuine equivalence).
- **Cost**: ~60–120 LOC, low–medium risk. Comparable to — but smaller and lower-risk than — the 01I8
  sheaf-infra route that overran (this is one equivalence + one `Ext`-functor, no cover-dense / site
  plumbing).
- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** (sound; the isoSpec tool genuinely belongs here, on the *whole* affine).

## Recommendation

**Split the residual into the two transports it actually needs and route each correctly:**

1. **need #2 — general-affine-open vanishing — do it AMBIENT, by enlarging `B`.** Generalize
   `standard_cover_cofinal` and `affine_surj_of_vanishing` from `D f` to a general affine open (swap in
   `IsAffineOpen.isCompact`), set `affineCoverSystem`'s `B := {affine opens}` (and `Cov`'s covered set
   to affine opens), then read off
   `affine_serre_vanishing_allAffineOpens : ∀ V affine, ∀ q≥1, Ext^q_{(Spec R).Modules}(jShriekOU V, H)=0`
   from `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis (affineCoverSystem R) … V hV`. ~40–80 LOC, no new Mathlib gap.

2. **need #1 — abstract affine `U` → `Spec Γ(U)` — do it with `isoSpec` (the SOUND use of the tool).**
   Assemble `Φ : U.Modules ≌ (Spec Γ U).Modules` from `Scheme.Modules.pushforward U.isoSpec.hom/.inv`
   + `pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardId`/`pushforwardCongr`; transport the step-1 vanishing back to `U`
   and `V = j⁻¹W` via `Ext.mapExactFunctor` (+ `jShriekOU`/quasi-coherence naturality). ~60–120 LOC.

**Do NOT** transport along `j⁻¹W ≅ Spec Γ(j⁻¹W)` to make `V` the whole space — that is the
restriction-preserves-injectives wall the project already rejected. `isoSpec` is the right tool only
for the *whole* affine `U`, never for the proper open `V`.

The `rightDerivedNatIso`/`sectionsFunctorCorepIso` already in the file remain correct and reusable;
they are orthogonal to this choice.

8973	    \operatorname{Ext}^q_{\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U}\bigl(j_!\mathcal{O}_{V'},\, \mathcal{H}'\bigr),
8974	  \]
8975	  where \(V'\) is the affine open image of \(V\) under the isomorphism and \(\mathcal{H}'\) is the
8976	  transported (still quasi-coherent) coefficient. In particular the residual over \(U\) vanishes iff
8977	  its transport over \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma U\) does.
8978	\end{lemma}

8983	  gives a genuine isomorphism of schemes \(\varphi : U \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_U)\).

8992	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_mapExactFunctor_mathlib}) applied to \(\Phi\) and to its quasi-inverse gives
8993	  mutually inverse maps on \(\operatorname{Ext}\), hence the displayed isomorphism. It remains to

13	/-!
14	# Map between Ext groups induced by an exact functor

16	In this file, we define the map `Ext^k (M, N) → Ext^k (F(M), F(N))`,
17	where `F` is an exact functor between abelian categories.

21	* `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mapExactFunctor` : The map between `Ext` induced by
22	  `CategoryTheory.LocalizerMorphism.smallShiftedHomMap`.

24	* `CategoryTheory.Functor.mapExtAddHom` : Upgraded of `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mapExactFunctor`
25	  into an additive homomorphism.

27	* `CategoryTheory.Functor.mapExtLinearMap` : Upgrade of `F.mapExtAddHom` assuming `F` is linear.

16	/-!
17	
18	# Bijections Between Ext
19	
20	In this file, we show that the maps between `Ext` induced
21	by a fully faithful exact functor `F : C ⥤ D` are bijective when either
22	1. `F` preserves projective objects and `C` has enough projectives, or
23	2. `F` preserves injective objects and `C` has enough injectives.

  CategoryTheory.Equivalence.mk (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom) (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.inv)
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).symm ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.hom_inv_id.symm ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.hom φ.inv).symm)
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.inv φ.hom ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.inv_hom_id ≪≫
      Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y)

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ext` transport along the spectrum equivalence (Need #1)

The whole-scheme spectrum isomorphism `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`, a genuine iso because
`U` is affine) induces an *equivalence* of module categories `U.Modules ≌ (Spec Γ U).Modules`,
assembled from `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` along the iso and its coherences. An equivalence functor
is fully faithful, exact, and preserves injective objects, so
`Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects` upgrades `Ext.mapExactFunctor` along it to an
*isomorphism* of `Ext`-groups. This transports the residual general-affine-open Serre vanishing for
`U` to the `Spec`-developed `affine_serre_vanishing_general_open`.

The pushforward-of-an-iso construction is generic in the scheme isomorphism `φ : X ≅ Y`. -/

/-- **A scheme isomorphism induces an equivalence of module categories.** For `φ : X ≅ Y`, the
pushforward `Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom` is an equivalence with quasi-inverse the pushforward
along `φ.inv`; the unit/counit are supplied by the `pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr`
coherences. Project-local: Mathlib packages the pushforward functor and its coherences but not the
equivalence assembled from an iso (the genuine equivalence used to transport `Ext` for Need #1). -/
noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
    X.Modules ≌ Y.Modules :=
  CategoryTheory.Equivalence.mk
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom) (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.inv)
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).symm ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.hom_inv_id.symm ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.hom φ.inv).symm)
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.inv φ.hom ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.inv_hom_id ≪≫
      Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y)

/-- The functor of `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` is (definitionally) the additive `pushforward φ.hom`. -/
noncomputable instance pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.Additive :=
  inferInstanceAs (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom).Additive

/-- **`Ext` transport along a scheme isomorphism.** For `φ : X ≅ Y` and `A B : X.Modules`, the
equivalence `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` (whose functor is fully faithful, exact, and injective-object
preserving) upgrades the additive map `Ext.mapExactFunctor` to an isomorphism of `Ext`-groups
`Ext A B n ≃+ Ext (φ_* A) (φ_* B) n`, via `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects`.
Project-local: the `Ext`-isomorphism half of Need #1 (the spectrum-equivalence transport). -/
noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
    [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (A B : X.Modules) (n : ℕ) :
    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ≃+
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj A)
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj B) n :=
  AddEquiv.ofBijective ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.mapExtAddHom A B n)
    (Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor A B n)

/-- **Need #1 — `Ext` transport along the whole-scheme spectrum equivalence.** For an affine scheme
`U`, the canonical iso `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`) induces, for every `q`, an isomorphism
`Ext^q_U(A, B) ≅ Ext^q_{Spec Γ U}(φ_* A, φ_* B)`. In particular the residual `Ext` over `U` vanishes
iff its transport over `Spec Γ U` does. Project-local target of `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`.

The remaining identification of the transported arguments — `φ_*(jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU V'` for
`V'` the (affine) image open, and quasi-coherence of `φ_* H` — is handed off (see the task-result
file); it is orthogonal to this transport and is the geometric naturality of `j_!` under a scheme
iso. -/
noncomputable def modulesIsoSpecExtTransport (U : Scheme.{u}) [IsAffine U]
    [EnoughInjectives U.Modules] (A B : U.Modules) (n : ℕ) :
    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ≃+
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso U.isoSpec).functor.obj A)
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso U.isoSpec).functor.obj B) n :=
  Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv U.isoSpec A B n

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/OpenImmersionPushforward.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (4, all axiom-clean):**
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso` (def, ~line 203)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive` (instance, ~line 213)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv` (def, ~line 230)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.modulesIsoSpecExtTransport` (def, ~line 250) — the named Lane-3 target
- **Declarations blocked (0):** none attempted-and-failed. The hard hand-off piece (`jShriekOU`
  naturality under a scheme iso) was deliberately NOT attempted as a `sorry`-free decl — it is a
  genuine multi-iter build, scoped precisely below.
- **Sorry count in file:** before = 2 (lines 306/372, the `_acyclic`/`_comp` residuals), after = 2
  (now lines 317/415 after insert; UNCHANGED — left as-is per the partial-progress bar, they wait on
  Lanes 1+2 outputs + the argument-image identification, assembled next iter).
- **Build:** `lake env lean` GREEN (only the 2 pre-existing residual sorries warn). All 4 new decls
  verified axiom-clean via `lean_verify` → `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

## modulesIsoSpecExtTransport (RESOLVED — Lane-3 core)
- **Approach:** The directive's planned route, executed. The clean vehicle turned out to be
  `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects`
  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/MapBijective.lean:65`), NOT a hand-built
  functor-composition lemma for `Ext.mapExactFunctor` (which does NOT exist in Mathlib and would have
  been a deep gap). That Mathlib lemma states: a **fully faithful, exact, injective-object-preserving**
  functor `F` over a category with **enough injectives** induces a **bijective** `F.mapExtAddHom`.
- **Construction chain:**
  1. `pushforwardEquivOfIso (φ : X ≅ Y) : X.Modules ≌ Y.Modules` — `Equivalence.mk` from
     `Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom` / `…φ.inv`, with unit/counit assembled from the verified
     coherences `pushforwardId` / `pushforwardComp` / `pushforwardCongr` (+ `φ.hom_inv_id` /
     `φ.inv_hom_id`). No coherence proof obligation — `Equivalence.mk` adjusts the counit itself.
  2. An equivalence functor is automatically `Full`, `Faithful`, `PreservesFiniteLimits`,
     `PreservesFiniteColimits`, and `PreservesInjectiveObjects` (all `inferInstance`). The only
     instance not auto-found is `.functor.Additive` (the `Equivalence.mk` projection is defeq to but
     not syntactically `pushforward φ.hom`); supplied by the global instance
     `pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive := inferInstanceAs (pushforward φ.hom).Additive`.
  3. `pushforwardExtAddEquiv` = `AddEquiv.ofBijective (…functor.mapExtAddHom A B n)
     (mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects …)`.
  4. `modulesIsoSpecExtTransport U := pushforwardExtAddEquiv U.isoSpec` — instantiation at the
     whole-scheme spectrum iso `Scheme.isoSpec : U ≅ Spec Γ(U,⊤)` (`[IsAffine U]`).
- **`HasExt`:** re-activated the project-local `attribute [local instance] hasExtModules` (same idiom
  as `AffineSerreVanishing.lean:30` / `CechToCohomology.lean:24`) to avoid the slow
  `HasSmallLocalizedHom` search (it timed out at 20k heartbeats without this).
- **Hypothesis:** the transport requires `[EnoughInjectives U.Modules]` (the source-category enough-
  injectives condition of `mapExt_bijective`). The consumer `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic`
  carries `[HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]`; the `HasInjectiveResolutions → EnoughInjectives`
  connector (~6 LOC, `InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι`, already a flagged next-iter task in
  PROGRESS) must be wired at the consumer site. `EnoughInjectives` is NOT auto-derived from
  `HasInjectiveResolutions` (checked — `inferInstance` fails).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## Hand-off: argument-image identification (NOT added — genuine multi-iter build)
To actually discharge the line-317 `_acyclic` residual via this transport, the consumer needs, on top
of the `Ext`-iso just built:
1. **`jShriekOU` naturality under a scheme iso (HARD, the real gap):**
   `(pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU (φ.hom ''ᵁ V)`.
   `jShriekOU V = sheafification.obj (free.obj (yoneda.obj V))`, so this requires commuting
   `Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom` with the free/sheafification adjunction and relating
   `yoneda.obj V` on `Opens X` to `yoneda.obj (φ.hom ''ᵁ V)` on `Opens Y` via the homeomorphism `φ`.
   This is "`j_!` is carried along a scheme iso" — estimated multi-iter; the deepest piece of Need #1.
2. **Image-open affineness (TRIVIAL — inline, do not wrap):** for `V` affine,
   `IsAffineOpen (φ.hom ''ᵁ V)` is `(Scheme.Hom.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion φ.hom).mpr hV`
   (`φ.hom` is an open immersion since it is an iso). One-liner; left for the consumer to avoid
   blueprint debt for a trivial fact.
3. **Quasi-coherence preservation (MEDIUM):** `(pushforward φ.hom).obj H` quasi-coherent when `H` is.
   `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` is over a generic site/ring sheaf; pushforward along `φ.hom`
   changes the base ring sheaf `X.ringCatSheaf → Y.ringCatSheaf`, so this is a transport along the
   iso of ringed spaces — non-trivial, no off-the-shelf Mathlib instance found.

Once (1)–(3) land, the line-317 residual `IsZero ((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W))))
.rightDerived q).obj H)` over the affine `U = j⁻¹W` is discharged by: transport the `Ext` to
`Spec Γ U` via `modulesIsoSpecExtTransport`, rewrite the first argument to `jShriekOU V'` (V' affine)
via (1)+(2), and apply `AffineSerreVanishing.affine_serre_vanishing_general_open` there (Lane 2 / the
`htilde` seed from Lane 1). The `_acyclic` (line 317) and `_comp` (line 415) sorries are intentionally
left untouched — they are the assembly site, gated on Lanes 1+2 + the hand-off above.

## Needs blueprint entry
All 4 new non-private declarations are infrastructure with no blueprint block yet (the blueprint's
`lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport` currently points `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.modulesIsoSpecExtTransport_TODO}`,
a placeholder name). Planner/reviewer should:
- **Re-point** `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`'s `\lean{...}` from the `_TODO` placeholder to the
  real `AlgebraicGeometry.modulesIsoSpecExtTransport`, and mark `\leanok` (handled by `sync_leanok`).
- **Add blueprint nodes** (currently `lean_aux`, will show in `archon dag-query unmatched`) for the
  three supporting decls — or bundle their names into the `\lean{...}` of `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`:
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso` — relies on
    `Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId/pushforwardComp/pushforwardCongr` + `Equivalence.mk`.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive` — `inferInstanceAs` (defeq).
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv` — relies on
    `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects` + equivalence-functor instances
    (`Full`/`Faithful`/`PreservesFiniteLimits`/`PreservesFiniteColimits`/`PreservesInjectiveObjects`)
    + `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` + `hasExtModules`.
- **Note** the build target name is now real (drop the `% NOTE: build target … does not exist yet`
  comment for this lemma).

## Why I stopped
**Real progress:** 4 axiom-clean declarations added (the complete module-category-equivalence +
`Ext`-transport core of Need #1, exactly matching the partial-progress bar "build the module-category
equivalence + the `Ext.mapExactFunctor` step"):
- `Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso` (~L203)
- `pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive` (~L213)
- `Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv` (~L230)
- `modulesIsoSpecExtTransport` (~L250)

I stopped because the next ingredient — the `jShriekOU`-image identification under a scheme iso
(hand-off item 1) — is a genuine multi-iter homological/sheaf build (commuting pushforward with the
free/sheafification adjunction + yoneda transport across the homeomorphism), which the partial bar
explicitly authorized to hand off. The two smaller hand-off items (image affineness = a verified
one-liner; quasi-coherence preservation) are documented for the consumer. No external-LLM key is set
in env (`env | grep` empty), so no informal-agent consult was possible; routes were found via Mathlib
source scan (`MapBijective.lean` was the decisive find). No approaches were written-but-unattempted.

Need#1 (`OpenImmersionPushforward.lean`, iter-057) `Ext` transport along `Scheme.isoSpec` is DONE
axiom-clean: `Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso` (equivalence from a scheme iso φ, via
`Equivalence.mk` + `pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr`), `pushforwardExtAddEquiv`
(the `Ext` AddEquiv), `modulesIsoSpecExtTransport` (instantiation at `U.isoSpec`).

**KEY FINDING:** `Abelian.Ext.mapExactFunctor` has NO functor-composition lemma in Mathlib (no
`mapExactFunctor (F⋙G) = mapExactFunctor G ∘ mapExactFunctor F`), so you CANNOT build the Ext-iso by
composing functor + inverse. Instead use `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects`
(`Mathlib/.../Ext/MapBijective.lean`): a fully faithful + exact + injective-object-preserving functor
over a category with `[EnoughInjectives]` has bijective `mapExtAddHom` → wrap with
`AddEquiv.ofBijective`. An equivalence functor auto-supplies Full/Faithful/PreservesFiniteLimits/
PreservesFiniteColimits/PreservesInjectiveObjects; only `.functor.Additive` needs `inferInstanceAs
(pushforward φ.hom).Additive` (Equivalence.mk projection not syntactically the pushforward).

Needs `attribute [local instance] hasExtModules` (else HasSmallLocalizedHom search times out @20k hb)
and hypothesis `[EnoughInjectives U.Modules]` (NOT auto-derived from `[HasInjectiveResolutions]` —
the ~6 LOC connector must be wired at consumer site).

RESIDUAL hand-off (to discharge the `_acyclic` line-317 sorry next iter): (1) HARD multi-iter =
`jShriekOU` naturality `pushforward φ.hom .obj (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU (φ.hom ''ᵁ V)` (commute
pushforward with free/sheafification adjunction + yoneda across homeo); (2) trivial inline =
`(Scheme.Hom.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion φ.hom).mpr hV` for V' affine; (3) medium =
quasi-coherence preserved under pushforward-along-iso. Then transport + rewrite arg + apply
`affine_serre_vanishing_general_open`. See [[openimm-acyclic-reduced]].

I've completed Lane 3 (Need #1) for this iteration. Here's the summary:

## What I built (4 axiom-clean declarations)

In `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/OpenImmersionPushforward.lean`:

1. **`Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso`** — a scheme iso `φ : X ≅ Y` induces an equivalence of module categories `X.Modules ≌ Y.Modules`, assembled from `pushforward φ.hom`/`φ.inv` + the `pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr` coherences.
2. **`pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive`** — the defeq additivity instance (the only instance not auto-found).
3. **`Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv`** — the `Ext`-isomorphism `Ext A B n ≃+ Ext (φ_* A) (φ_* B) n`.
4. **`modulesIsoSpecExtTransport`** — the named Lane-3 target, instantiation at `U.isoSpec`.

All verified axiom-clean (`[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`) via `lean_verify`, with an authoritative `lake env lean` build passing (only the 2 pre-existing residual sorries remain, intentionally untouched).

## Key finding (recorded to memory + handoff)

The directive expected `Ext.mapExactFunctor` plumbing, but Mathlib has **no functor-composition lemma** for it — so the equivalence-functor-then-inverse route is a dead end. Instead I found `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects` (in `Ext/MapBijective.lean`): a fully faithful, exact, injective-preserving functor over a category with enough injectives has a **bijective** `mapExtAddHom`. An equivalence functor satisfies all of these for free, so `AddEquiv.ofBijective` finishes it cleanly. This is the decisive, cheaper route.

## Handed off (per the partial-progress bar)

The argument-image identification: the **hard** `jShriekOU` naturality under a scheme iso (a genuine multi-iter sheaf build), plus image-affineness (a verified one-liner, left inline) and quasi-coherence preservation. These + Lanes 1/2 feed the `_acyclic`/`_comp` assembly next iter. The task-result file has the precise decomposition and the blueprint-debt note (re-point the `_TODO` placeholder name).
